q3.explanation: RSI-14 (14-period relative strength index) is upstream QuantMuse's own real FactorCalculator.calculate_technical_factors() output for AAPL as of yfinance's most recent 1-year daily history at call time. No upstream-native composite/aggregate exists across FactorCalculator's ~13 real technical/momentum readings (calculate_all_factors() only concatenates per-family dicts, no combination logic of its own) -- per CLAUDE.md's fabrication rule this adapter does not invent a weighted average across them. RSI-14 was chosen as the single `values` entry because it is bounded to a fixed [0,100] scale and is upstream's own canonical standalone technical reading (unlike MACD, which needs a signal-line crossover, or price_vs_ma*, which needs a peer baseline, to be meaningful alone). All other real readings (macd/macd_signal/macd_histogram, ma_20/50/200, price_vs_ma20/50/200, bb_upper/bb_lower/bb_position, momentum_{20,60,252}d, momentum_accel_*) are attached as evidence, not discarded. direction/strength are left unset: applying RSI's conventional 70/30 overbought/oversold thresholds to derive a LONG/SHORT call would be a new adapter-authored interpretive layer, not verified real upstream output. Known limitation: yfinance's period='1y' price fetch is relative to wall-clock call time, not point-in-time as of 2024-01-15 (same limitation already present in this adapter's Q2 technical-factor usage before this recovery).

fusion_risk_factors: Concentration risk: single stock (AAPL) comprises 100% of the portfolio, amplifying idiosyncratic risk.
Overbought technical conditions: RSI at 88.6 (above 70) and Bollinger Band position at 0.92 (near upper band) indicate elevated short-term reversal risk.
Negative sentiment: weighted sentiment -0.35, though low confidence (0.45) and small sample size (3) limit reliability.
Momentum acceleration: both 20-day and 60-day momentum accelerate, increasing vulnerability to a sharp correction.
Price far above moving averages (20% above 200-day MA), suggesting stretched valuations and potential mean reversion.
